title: France into World Cup semi-final win over Morocco
subhead: Kylian Mbappé shrugged off an early penalty miss to score and set up another goal as France beat Morocco 2-0 on Thursday to book a place in the World Cup semi-finals.

title: After a year eyeing France's presidency, Bardella has to settle for deputy

Burnham on track to become next UK PM as hundreds of Labour MPs back leadership bid
subhead: Labour's most popular politician, Andy Burnham – nicknamed the 'King of the North' for winning three consecutive Greater Manchester mayoral elections – has vowed to bring about a significant rebalance of power in the UK. In the absence of a contest, Burnham will be crowned Labour leader and prime minister in waiting on July 17.

title: 'Imperial obsession and reckless escalation: Vladimir Putin has never appeared so trapped by his fantasies'
subhead: Defeated in his attempt to subjugate Ukraine by capturing Kyiv in early 2022 and unable, four years later, to conquer all the Ukrainian plains bordering Russia's western border, the Russian leader has taken revenge on civilian populations, says Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: France or Morocco? The World Cup dilemma for Paris's Moroccan community
subhead: Les Bleus and the Atlas Lions face off on Thursday for a spot in the football tournament's semifinals. In the French capital, some fans have already made their decision. For others, choosing a side is proving far more complicated.

title: Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
subhead: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.

Two Israeli NGOs warn of a 'de facto annexation at an unprecedented pace' in the West Bank
subhead: Israel now controls 18% of the occupied territories, compared to 7% before the war in Gaza. A report from the non-profit organizations Peace Now and Kerem Navot has been documenting the acceleration of land grabs, the construction of settler-only roads and the expulsion of local communities over the past three and a half years.

title: The day Marine Le Pen took back control of her fate, despite a conviction
subhead: After being sentenced to one year of house arrest, the French far right's leader found an opening back into the presidential race.

title: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
subhead: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.
